rules.name: python-script-logging
rules.description: All Python scripts and tools MUST implement logging
rules.rationale: Centralized logging enables debugging, audit trails, and operational visibility

required: Log to project logs/ or monorepo logs/

enforcement.check: All .py files in shared/tools/ and bin/ must import logging

examples.code:
import logging
from pathlib import Path

LOG_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / 'logs' / 'tools'
LOG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
LOG_FILE = LOG_DIR / 'rlhf_tracker.log'

logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format='%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
    handlers=[
        logging.FileHandler(LOG_FILE),
        logging.StreamHandler()  # Also log to console
    ]
)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

import logging
from pathlib import Path

# Assume running from project root
LOG_DIR = Path.cwd() / 'logs'
LOG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
LOG_FILE = LOG_DIR / 'deployment.log'

logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
    handlers=[logging.FileHandler(LOG_FILE)]
)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

rules.name: log-location-standard
rules.description: Logs MUST go to standardized locations
rules.rationale: Predictable log locations enable troubleshooting and log aggregation

required: Create logs/ directory if it doesn't exist
Use script/service name as log filename

enforcement.check: Log files must be in logs/ directory

rules.name: log-format-standard
rules.description: Use consistent log format across all scripts
rules.rationale: Consistent formatting enables log parsing and aggregation

enforcement.check: Log format includes asctime and levelname

rules.name: log-levels-usage
rules.rationale: Correct log levels enable filtering and alerting

levels.DEBUG: Detailed diagnostic information (function calls, variable values)
levels.INFO: General informational messages (script started, file processed)
levels.WARNING: Something unexpected but handled (deprecated usage, fallback applied)
levels.ERROR: Error occurred but script continues (file not found, API failed)
levels.CRITICAL: Severe error, script cannot continue (config missing, auth failed)

recommended: Use ERROR for failures that don't stop execution

examples.code:
logger.info('Processing file: %s', filepath)
logger.warning('Config not found, using defaults')
logger.error('Failed to upload to S3: %s', error)
logger.critical('Database connection failed, exiting')

rules.name: log-rotation
rules.description: Consider log rotation for long-running services

examples.code:
from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler

handler = RotatingFileHandler(
    LOG_FILE,
    maxBytes=10*1024*1024,  # 10MB
    backupCount=5
)
logger.addHandler(handler)

LOG-002.message: Log file not in logs/ directory

LOG-003.fix: Use standard format with asctime and levelname
